{{User:LV-CRzhan-hun/Object Class|from= ''Paper Candy'' |name=Paper Candy|class=2|number=93|commonness=Low|scene=General|demand=Medium|text=#add8e6|border=#ee82ee}} ==Description== Paper candy is a substance described as a collection of paper strips that resemble ribbon paper or come in various unusual colors. They are often found on tables or in fruit bowls in a peculiar manner. While these objects may look like regular paper scraps, they are significantly heavier than typical paper pieces and closer in mass to small candies.
 Paper Candy, as candy-like object, typically features a blend of fruit flavors reminiscent of wood pulp and comes in various colors ranging from red to purple. Recent research indicates that Paper Candy may exhibit colors imperceptible to the human eye on its surface. Moreover, specific experiments have demonstrated heightened color sensitivity to Paper Candy among many animals and certain intelligent entities such as [[Entity 9|Facelings]] and [[Pranksters]]. It is important to note that Paper Candy of the same color often offers different flavors, which can pose a challenge in selecting one that matches personal preference.
 The taste of Paper Candy that has been documented so far is as follows: {{Collapse|num=tangguo|initial=
♦
|final= |body= {{User:LV-CRzhan-hun/Smelling Page Of Paper Candy}} }} ==Characteristic== {{Blurimage|file=Paper Candy.webp|caption=A bowl of red and orange Paper candy}} The structural characteristics of Paper Candy resemble those of a streamer in terms of texture, appearance, and tactile properties, as previously discussed. However, it deviates significantly from standard paper as it exists exclusively within the Backrooms. From a clinical perspective, direct consumption of Paper Candy ''does not appear to carry any adverse consequences''. Report shows a sensation of coolness and a refreshing breeze throughout the body and mind while enjoying its flavor. For the most part, Paper Candy is primarily refreshed in relatively empty and confined environments. It often manifests in the form of "scraps of paper in a fruit bowl" and is typically placed on a wooden table. This particular table typically emits a subtle scent of sandalwood, which attracts both entities and humans on a pheromone level. The smell is considered ''harmless'' and even serves as a sign of safety, as it indicates the presence of nearby exits. Interestingly, it is impossible to move the bowl or table that holding Paper Candy of naturally generated. After a while, it will spontaneously combust, leaving behind a strong smell of sawdust. Paper Candy, when consumed, is usually able to randomly alleviate any discomfort currently experienced by the wanderer's body, or counteract an effect that is currently affecting them, or even a minor illness. However, based on the current experiment, it appears that Paper Candy's ability to counteract these effects is completely random and dependent on the physical constitution of the person consuming it, with no discernible pattern. Additionally, when it comes to entities, Paper Candy has varying effects. Some entities show great interest in Paper Candy, often engaging in excessive consumption or intense sucking, while others completely ignore Paper Candy and any beings covered by it, for reasons that remain unknown. This behavior has long been regarded as a unique secret among sapient entities. Normally, eating Paper Candy doesn't cause discomfort, but its unique taste will entice most people to try it. ==Source== Paper Candy, classified as a type of confectionery, is produced in an unconventional manner—not through artificial means nor direct manufacture by any entity. Several experiments have indicated that Paper Candy exhibits a form of contagion. The specifics of this contagion are still under investigation, and its effects and characteristics remain unknown; however, certain observable phenomena have been identified. Furthermore, it has been discovered that pieces of paper containing Element "Ht" will instantly and autonomously transform into Paper Candy. Specifically, paper strips made from wood pulp containing Element "Ht" initiate this transformation upon contact. Similarly, a compound of "Ht" mixed with a paper strip causes the strip to emit a sound resembling "boiling popcorn" and gradually transform into Paper Candy. Notably, no chemical reactions have been observed under submicroscopic conditions; only the sound production occurs, defined as "Paper Candy's Type II infection." Confetti affected by this infection demonstrates identical characteristics to Paper Candy. It has been found that pieces of paper containing Element "Ht" will immediately and spontaneously turn into Paper Candy, that is, pieces of paper made from wood pulp containing Element "Ht" will immediately cause them to turn into Paper Candy. Compound of "Ht" mixed with a strip of paper will also cause the strip of paper to spontaneously make a sound similar to "boiling popcorn", which will gradually transform into Paper Candy. However, under the current submicroscopic conditions, no chemical reaction has been observed, only the production of sound, which is considered to be "Paper Candy's Type II infection", and the confetti infected by this contagion effect has exactly the same effect as Paper Candy. In some cases, Compound "HtaHbOc·k Eg· (a-k) Sm·i Nt·a Ma· C(?)" is stable in Paper Candy but not in any other form of conjugation. It does not appear to carry any conjugators or elements found in other types of Paper Candy. In this scenario, Paper Candy will immediately undergo "glue fission" upon destruction, resulting in a large sponge of carbon. This effect is classified as "Paper Candy Type III infection," and Paper Candy affected by this contagion results in the formation of a lump of carbon. ==Recommended for consumers== For the consumption of Paper Candy, it should normally be{{Collapse|num=next|initial=''Do what you can''……|final=|body= ''Do what you can''……
Darling……You smell so great……

{{Collapse|num=b1|initial=|final=|body=
'''You Died?'''
With the sound of life breaking through the night sky, the girl's face instantly lost its vitality. You see a stream of colorful liquid spurting from her chest, like a stunning rainbow, and the pink and blue tears slipped from her eyes.
「My……Dear……Sister……」
You hurriedly push her away and stand up. She fell to the ground trembling, moaning, despair and pleading in her eyes, accompanied by a faint whimper.
「Sorry……」
Eventually, she closed her eyes, like a cyan flower, quietly withering in the night
Immediately afterward, the girl's body burst open, scattering rainbow-colored liquids all over the ground, and producing many items that resemble streamers.
「Be careful.」
A pair of hands reach out from the shadows and pull you over.
You feel the warmth of your gloves, and before you can ask, you're immediately taken out of the level.
「To be honest」
「I didn't understand it all along」
「Why are they in such a big demand for Paper Candy.」
「Oh.」
「My bad.」
「I ignored you」
「You should have read the documentation she gave you. Haven't you?」
「That's half of the official documentation.」
「The remaining half was hidden by them.」
「But I have one more here.」
「Let's make do and see.」
{{Collapse|num=true|initial=''You pick up the rest of the file''|final=|body=
It's not so much that this candy is a treat; It's better to say that this paper candy is a disaster. Remember! Do not misuse Paper Candy! ==Paper Candy's benign/malignant effects== Considering the ingestion of Paper Candy purportedly has the capability to sporadically mitigate certain discomforts or ongoing effects observed in wanderers' bodies, it might appear to function akin to a medicinal remedy. However, it is presently strictly prohibited due to its malignant effects. In reality, Paper Candy does not eliminate the underlying effects; it merely masks them. The potent deceptive nature of Paper Candy conceals and accumulates the effects it influences, leading wanderers to believe it possesses therapeutic properties for corresponding ailments and effects. Consequently, in the short term, Paper Candy's sales and perceived value briefly surpassed that of [[Object 1|Almond Water]] during the same period. Nonetheless, Paper Candy actually has '''no therapeutic effects''' and may even exacerbate the effects it influences to some extent. However, its masking effect ensures that these effects do not manifest visibly. The inherent sweetness and unique flavor of Paper Candy contribute to severe dependency among its consumers. Regular consumption of Paper Candy results in diminished somatic and gustatory perception, while sensitivity to Paper Candy itself gradually intensifies. This effect drives wanderers to develop a profound dependence on Paper Candy in pursuit of realism and enhanced sensory experiences. Furthermore, prolonged cessation from Paper Candy consumption leads to reduced suppression of its effects, causing previously accumulated effects to resurface exponentially and forcibly accumulate within the body of wanderers. This results in severe distress and, in certain cases, potentially fatal outcomes. Compounding the severity is the inability to treat effects and ailments that remain concealed by Paper Candy, necessitating medical intervention only after manifestation. The unique characteristic of Paper Candy lies in its capacity to perceive '''death'''. When consumed by individuals in a moribund state, Paper Candy enhances hidden effects with a potent surge of energy, exponentially released within the wanderer's body, resulting in extreme agony. Records indicate that a consumer of Paper Candy experienced pain and isolation amplified to seven thousand times in their final moments, rendering them devoid of spatial awareness and leading to a distorted demise. In some observed instances, wanderers subjected to this effect have experienced bodily explosions shortly after death, generating numerous instances of Paper Candy or Sponge Carbon. Currently, there are no effective medications specifically targeting Paper Candy, and treatment often involves concentrated isolation. However, the effects generated by Paper Candy are typically limited and depend on the quantity consumed by the wanderer. Once the consumption of Paper Candy reaches a certain threshold, it can lead to the concentration of Paper Candy's effects, therefore sheld "Psychic Barrier Drugs" and [[Object 1|Almond Water]], making the infection extremely challenging to cure. In such cases, the wanderer is generally considered to have transitioned into a '''Candy Stealer'''. =='''Candy Stealer'''== {{Blurimage|file=carbon brain.png|caption=A carbon brain image, uncomfortable part has been removed.}} As mentioned earlier, the emergence of Candy Stealers stems from individuals infected with the incurable Paper Candy condition. Their level of threat sometimes surpasses even certain hazardous entities due to their rationality and intelligence, because their appearance enables them to appear completely indistinguishable from normal humans. However, they occasionally exhibit distinctive features from typical human behavior. Currently, Candy Stealers cannot be classified as a distinct entity due to their physical resemblance to humans and their extreme behaviors aimed at proximity. Instead, they are considered a highly factionalized human group rather than an organization. Candy Stealer's presence itself is highly malignant. In certain circumstances, they are likely to deceive vagrants unaware of the effects of Paper Candy, inducing them to consume it and fostering dependence. As mentioned earlier, some users of Paper Candy experience bodily explosions upon death, initiating the formation of more Paper Candy. This process is a methodical slaughter of humans—transforming consumers into Paper Candy for further consumption through deceit for them. In extreme cases, Candy Stealers forcibly administer "Type III infectious Paper Candy" to vagrants, making the carbon brain formed from explosions in brain masses. Sometimes, Candy Stealers may cooperate with each other to "hunt" diners, but it has also been observed that some Candy Stealers imprison each other, causing the other to be unable to consume Paper Candy and resulting in death, and using this to consume Paper Candy produced from the other's body. The following is an interview record of Candy Stealer who was quarantined:
(Use MSID to represent a major testing organization, and PCI to represent testees)
Interview transcript |-|Test 1(Male, age: 17)=
MSID: Good morning, sir. How are you feeling? PCI:Bad morning, I'm not good, everyone is not good, I want to eat candy! MSID:Sir, please remain calm PCI:I want to eat candy, I want to eat candy! I want to eat candy!! Is that candy in your hand? What a big one, give me a try! MSID:Sir, if you continue to behave like this, I will fulfill the Hippocratic oath to treat you. Do you want medical treatment or physical therapy? PCI:Okay, great... I'll stop... but have you seen the candyland? I really want to take a bite MSID:shxx, this is completely impossible to communicate with ''Testee suddenly fell into a state of madness and screamed in agony on the ground. Then, the research institute fell into darkness, and the door was violently opened. Testee was dragged away from the institute by a [[Entity 3|smiler]].'' ''Test ended due to unexpected circumstances''
|-|Test 2(Female, Age: 8)=
MSID:Seriously? Is this kid still eating Paper Candy? Where did you find her? M.E.G.:I don't know. She's just a suspected Candy Stealer, so I brought her in. ''Later, Testee suddenly lying in front of the glass, shedding pink tears and pleading.'' PCI:Dear! please... neka's body is so itchy and pain... Wanna Candy! ''At this point, M.E.G. tester immediately became nervous.'' M.E.G.:She can't beg so much, she can't eat anymore, but I still can't bear to see her pitiful appearance. MSID:Have you ever tried the method of mixing glutinous rice paper with syrup? M.E.G.:Nah, I haven't experimented yet, I'll go get some. ''About 2 minutes later, the researcher at M.E.G. took the syrup glutinous rice paper and handed it to the MSID personnel.'' ''At this moment, a dark blue [[Prankster]] appeared from the wall, and the testee appeared extremely nervous and hostile.'' Prankster:Stop, it's useless. ''The [[Prankster]] then integrated into the wall and left, but the MSID personnel ignored the [[Prankster]] and handed the sticky rice paper to the testee from the window.'' ''The testee immediately ate the glutinous rice paper but began to lower their head.'' PCI:My dear, you're lying to me... This candy is not delicious, it's not delicious! ''At this moment, the testee fell into a state of anger and violently hit the glass panel. In the panic of the research institute, the testee crawled out of the window and pulled M.E.G. The research institute collapsed to the ground.'' PCI:Your taste should be very sweet! ''Testee licks M.E.G. Researcher's face, attempting to drip her saliva into the researcher's mouth'' ''MSID attempted to calm the testee down using an anesthetic gun, but this seemed to be ineffective for the testee.'' ''In the end, a piece of Paper Candy was thrown from the wall, and the testee immediately rushed towards it and ate it, gradually calming down and being relocked in the isolation room.'' ''Test Ended''
==Anecdotes of Antidotes== Rumors have circulated suggesting that "brain carbon" or "brain-shaped sponge carbon" could serve as potential antidotes to Paper Candy, leading some vagrants to attempt bartering with Candy Stealers and consuming brains, resulting in varying degrees of casualties. However, it has been confirmed that "brain-shaped sponge carbon" is not an antidote but rather an enhanced form of Paper Candy, leading to more severe Paper Candy infections. These rumors are said to have originated from an unidentified substance abuse organization, though this has yet to be verified. {{Collapse|num=c|initial=Click to continue|final=|body=}}
